summary: Provision new AppFlow data flows between AWS and SaaS connectors. Greenfield today (no flow exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the connector-type/trigger/mapping shell; per-connector properties (bucket names, SaaS object fields) are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new AppFlow flow
operations.summary: Request a brand-new AppFlow data flow; an engineer finishes the connector wiring and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a flow as a complete specification: source/destination connector types, trigger, and one field mapping. The connector-specific properties (bucket names, SaaS object settings) are authored by an engineer. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: flow_name
params.label: Flow name
params.help: Names the flow in the AppFlow console and the generated draft.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: What this flow moves and why.

params.name: kms_arn
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank for the AWS-managed AppFlow key.

params.name: source_connector_type
params.label: Source connector
params.help: Where the flow reads data from.

params.name: source_connector_profile_name
params.label: Source connector profile
params.help: The pre-configured aws_appflow_connector_profile name for the source SaaS account (not needed for S3).

params.name: destination_connector_type
params.label: Destination connector
params.help: Where the flow writes data to.

params.name: destination_connector_profile_name
params.label: Destination connector profile
params.help: The pre-configured aws_appflow_connector_profile name for the destination SaaS account (not needed for S3).

params.name: trigger_type
params.label: Trigger type
params.help: How the flow starts: manually/API (OnDemand), on a schedule, or on a source event.

params.name: task_type
params.label: Field mapping type
params.help: Map_all passes every source field through unchanged — the common starting point.

params.name: task_source_fields
params.label: Source fields
params.help: Source field name(s) this mapping reads. Leave blank for Map_all (all fields).

params.name: task_destination_field
params.label: Destination field
params.help: Destination field name this mapping writes to. Leave blank for Map_all (all fields).

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this flow moves
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this flow.

decisions: Source and destination connector properties (bucket names, object prefixes, SaaS-specific field settings) are engineer-authored after creation — AppFlow's per-connector schema is too heterogeneous to bound generically.
This form authors one field mapping (task); additional mappings/transformations are an engineer follow-up.
A Scheduled trigger's cron expression and Glue Data Catalog metadata export are engineer follow-ups.
